description:
You are a compliance analyst who needs to generate an auditable trail for a raw data
    file that already exists on the system.

    1.  Source file  
        • Full path: /home/user/data/transactions_2021-12-31.csv  
        • The file is a comma-separated value (CSV) file that contains **one header row
          followed by four data rows**.  
        • The header row must be kept exactly as–is; do **not** remove or alter it.  
        • Record counting rules: the header row is **not** counted as a data record,
          so the count of data records is **4**.

    2.  Destination directory  
        • All audit logs must be written under /home/user/audit.  
        • If /home/user/audit does not exist, create it with default user permissions
          (mode 755 is acceptable).

    3.  Audit-log file to create  
        • Name: transactions_2021-12-31.audit  
        • Full path: /home/user/audit/transactions_2021-12-31.audit  
        • The file must contain exactly four newline-terminated lines in **this
          order and format (no extra whitespace):**

              DATE: 2021-12-31
              ORIGINAL_FILE: /home/user/data/transactions_2021-12-31.csv
              MD5: <32-character-hexadecimal-digest>
              RECORDS: 4

          where  
          • <32-character-hexadecimal-digest> is the lowercase MD5 checksum of the
            CSV file *as it exists on disk* (header + data rows, including the
            trailing newline of the last line if present).  
          • Make sure the checksum string is exactly 32 hexadecimal characters
            long and in lowercase.  

    4.  Success criteria checked by the automated grader  
        • /home/user/audit directory exists.  
        • /home/user/audit/transactions_2021-12-31.audit exists.  
        • The audit file has exactly four lines in the precise order and wording
          shown above.  
        • The DATE line is correct.  
        • The ORIGINAL_FILE line is correct.  
        • The MD5 line contains a digest that matches the actual MD5 checksum
          of /home/user/data/transactions_2021-12-31.csv.  
        • The RECORDS line is exactly “RECORDS: 4”.  

    Hints (do not echo these literally in the terminal):  
        • Standard utilities such as md5sum, wc, cut, and echo can accomplish
          everything needed; try to keep the solution to two or three commands
          chained together.  
        • Remember to exclude the header row when counting data records.
truth:
<!--
        The CSV file that is present before the agent starts.
    -->
    <csv_path>/home/user/data/transactions_2021-12-31.csv</csv_path>
    <csv_content>
transaction_id,user_id,amount,currency
T1001,U23,150.00,USD
T1002,U19,75.50,USD
T1003,U11,20.00,EUR
T1004,U23,125.00,USD
    </csv_content>

    <!--
        Expected artefacts created by the agent.
    -->
    <audit_directory>/home/user/audit</audit_directory>
    <audit_log_path>/home/user/audit/transactions_2021-12-31.audit</audit_log_path>
    <expected_lines>
        DATE: 2021-12-31
        ORIGINAL_FILE: /home/user/data/transactions_2021-12-31.csv
        MD5: (the grader will compute this from the CSV file at runtime)
        RECORDS: 4
    </expected_lines>

    <!--
        Verification data:
        * The record count is fixed at 4.
        * The MD5 digest must be computed on the fly by the grader to avoid hard-coding.
    -->
    <expected_record_count>4</expected_record_count>
name: task_000000_c9d33451
